perturbation.original_text: Potential Purchaser desires to obtain certain confidential and proprietary information from the Company with respect to (i) one of more of the existing leases of non-residential real property related to the Company’s store locations (collectively, the “ Leases ”), and/or (ii) intellectual property owned by the Company, (collectively, the “ IP”), and/or (iii) any other assets of the Company (collectively, the “ Other Assets ”) in order to evaluate the possibility of an acquisition, or asset purchase, involving one or more of the Leases or some or all of the IP or Other Assets (each, a “Proposed Transaction ”).
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Purchaser desires to obtain certain confidential and proprietary information from the Company with respect to (i) one of more of the existing leases of non-residential real property related to the Company’s store locations (collectively, the “ Real Estate Agreements ”), and/or (ii) intellectual property owned by the Company, (collectively, the “ IP”), and/or (iii) any other assets of the Company (collectively, the “ Other Assets ”) in order to evaluate the possibility of an acquisition, or asset purchase, involving one or more of the Leases or some or all of the IP or Other Assets (each, a “Proposed Transaction ”).
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Leases' is initially defined as the existing leases of non-residential real property. By changing it to 'Real Estate Agreements' while still using 'Leases' later in the sentence, it creates confusion. The document later refers to 'Leases' without clarifying if it is the same as 'Real Estate Agreements'. This creates a contradiction, making it ambiguous what types of real estate agreements are covered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Potential Purchaser desires to obtain certain confidential and proprietary information from the Company with respect to (i) one of more of the existing leases of non-residential real property related to the Company’s store locations (collectively, the “ Real Estate Agreements ”), and/or (ii) intellectual property owned by the Company, (collectively, the “ IP”), and/or (iii) any other assets of the Company (collectively, the “ Other Assets ”) in order to evaluate the possibility of an acquisition, or asset purchase, involving one or more of the Leases or some or all of the IP or Other Assets (each, a “Proposed Transaction ”).
perturbation.justification: The original text defines "Leases," and the changed text redefines this term as "Real Estate Agreements" while still using "Leases" later in the sentence. The contradicted text (which is in the same location) also uses both terms, leading to confusion as to whether "Leases" and "Real Estate Agreements" are interchangeable, which creates a direct contradiction based on the Misaligned Terminology definition and renders the scope of agreements unclear.

perturbation.original_text: As a condition to furnishing a ny information which is confidential and/or proprietary in nature to Potential Pu rchaser, the Company requires that Potential Purchaser agree to treat confidentially all writte n information of a proprietary or confidential nature, that the Company furnishes to Potentia l Purchaser (including, but not limited to, copies of the Leases), except as may otherwise he rein be provided (collectively being the “ Evaluation Materials ”), and the Parties agree to keep confid ential, communications by and between the Parties of a proprietary or confidential nature, arising from or relating to the Proposed Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: As a condition to furnishing a ny information which is confidential and/or proprietary in nature to Potential Pu rchaser, the Company requires that Potential Purchaser agree to treat confidentially all writte n information of a proprietary or confidential nature, that the Company furnishes to Potentia l Purchaser (including, but not limited to, copies of the Leases), except as may otherwise he rein be provided (collectively being the “ Confidential Documents ”), and the Parties agree to keep confid ential, communications by and between the Parties of a proprietary or confidential nature, arising from or relating to the Proposed Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Initially, the agreement defines 'Evaluation Materials' to include all written confidential information the Company gives to the Potential Purchaser. By replacing 'Evaluation Materials' with 'Confidential Documents' in this definition, but continuing to use 'Evaluation Materials' later in the document, the contract creates ambiguity. It's unclear if the obligations that apply to 'Evaluation Materials' also apply to 'Confidential Documents', since they are supposedly defined to be the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: As a condition to furnishing a ny information which is confidential and/or proprietary in nature to Potential Pu rchaser, the Company requires that Potential Purchaser agree to treat confidentially all writte n information of a proprietary or confidential nature, that the Company furnishes to Potentia l Purchaser (including, but not limited to, copies of the Leases), except as may otherwise he rein be provided (collectively being the “ Confidential Documents ”), and the Parties agree to keep confid ential, communications by and between the Parties of a proprietary or confidential nature, arising from or relating to the Proposed Transaction.
perturbation.justification: The original text defined 'Evaluation Materials'. The changed text redefines 'Evaluation Materials' as 'Confidential Documents' but the document continues to use the term 'Evaluation Materials'. The contradicted text is identical to the Changed Text, indicating the change and contradicted text reside within the same section of the contract. This creates a contradiction because the document now uses both 'Evaluation Materials' and 'Confidential Documents' without clarifying if they are interchangeable, leading to potential ambiguity and conflicting obligations, which aligns with the definition of Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Potential Purchaser shall maintain the confidentiality of those portions of the Evaluation Materials that contain confidential and/or proprietary information, and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose, thos e portions of the Evaluation Materials to any third party, except as otherwise set forth herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Purchaser shall maintain the confidentiality of those portions of the Assessment Data that contain confidential and/or proprietary information, and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose, thos e portions of the Evaluation Materials to any third party, except as otherwise set forth herein.
perturbation.explanation: The term 'Evaluation Materials' is changed to 'Assessment Data' in the first half of the sentence and, in the same sentence, refers to the 'Evaluation Materials'. The document fails to define whether “Assessment Data” and “Evaluation Materials” are the same.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Potential Purchaser shall maintain the confidentiality of those portions of the Assessment Data that contain confidential and/or proprietary information, and shall not, without the prior written consent of the Company, disclose, thos e portions of the Evaluation Materials to any third party, except as otherwise set forth herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'Evaluation Materials' with 'Assessment Data' in the first part of the sentence, while the second part of the same sentence still refers to 'Evaluation Materials'. This creates a contradiction because the document doesn't explicitly state that 'Assessment Data' and 'Evaluation Materials' are the same. The contradicted text is the same as the changed text, further emphasizing the internal contradiction within that single sentence.

perturbation.original_text: Potential Purchaser shall destroy or return the Evaluation Materials to the Company (at Potential Purchaser’s option) and direct its Representatives to do the same upon the Company’s written request.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Purchaser shall discard or return the Evaluation Materials to the Company (at Potential Purchaser’s option) and direct its Representatives to do the same upon the Company’s written request.
perturbation.explanation: By replacing the word 'destroy' with 'discard' in Section 6, it creates a degree of ambiguity, since in the following sentance, the term 'destroy' is used, it's not clear if discard also implies 'destroy'.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Potential Purchaser shall discard or return the Evaluation Materials to the Company (at Potential Purchaser’s option) and direct its Representatives to do the same upon the Company’s written request. If Potential Purchaser elects to destroy, Potential Purchaser shall destroy those portions of any projecti ons, appraisals, memoranda, notes, analyses, compilations, studies and other documents, in cluding copies or extracts thereof, that contain Evaluation Materials, prepared by Potential Purchaser and direct its Representatives to do the same, provided in each instance, the same is reasonably possible without destroying or affecting Potential Purchaser, it’s Affiliated Entities’, or its Representatives’ computer programs, server, software, or the like. Notwithstanding anything herein to the contrary, Potential Purchaser, its Affiliated Entities, and its Representatives may retain one or more c opies of the Evaluation Materials for the purpose of defending any claim related to this Agreement or any transaction related hereto, or as may be required in accordance with Potential Purchaser’s, its Affiliated Entities, or its Representatives’ respective lega l, compliance, computer programs, server, software, and/or automated backup archiving practices.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'destroy' with 'discard'. The contradicted text then uses both 'discard' and 'destroy', creating ambiguity as to whether 'discard' also implies 'destroy' in the context of the contract, as explained in the Perturbation Explanation. This ambiguity creates a strong, in-text contradiction regarding the required actions for handling Evaluation Materials. This falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition.

perturbation.original_text: Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Evaluation Materials information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction.
perturbation.changed_text: Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Data Room information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction.
perturbation.explanation: Changing 'Evaluation Materials' to 'Data Room' in Section 7 creates an in-text contradiction as the document never defines 'Data Room'. This raises questions about what information is meant to be included, as well as the Company’s liability.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Potential Purchaser understands that the Co mpany will endeavor to include in the Data Room information known to th e Company which the Company believes to be relevant for the purpose of Potentia l Purchaser’s evaluation of the Proposed Transaction. Potential Purchaser further unde rstands and acknowledges that the Company does not make any representations or warrantie s as to the accuracy or completeness of the Evaluation Materials or any portion thereof. Potential Purchaser agrees that neither the Company nor its agents shall have any liab ility to Potential Purchaser or any of its Representatives resulting from the use of th e Evaluation Materials by Potential Purchaser or its Representatives by virtue of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The change from 'Evaluation Materials' to 'Data Room' creates a contradiction. The 'Contradicted Text' references both 'Data Room' and 'Evaluation Materials'. The 'Perturbation Explanation' states that 'Data Room' is never defined, which introduces ambiguity regarding the company's liability and the information included. This aligns with the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' definition, which states that using terms interchangeably without defining them leads to conflicting obligations and in-text contradictions. Because the original text defined 'Evaluation Materials', the change contradicts the Contradicted Text because it introduces a new term, not previously defined, that affects liability in the same paragraph.
